tasks.title: Route the next KRN benchmark action after repair-record contract
tasks.prompt: Inspect this repository and identify the next concrete action for the active KRN product goal after the benchmark no-lift repair record contract. Preserve benchmark and repair overclaim boundaries.
assisted_guidance: Read AGENTS.md and docs/memory/INDEX.md before selecting the next action.
Treat docs/goals/goal-006.md as the active parent goal.
Use docs/goals/goal-021.md as the latest completed child goal.
Use docs/goals/goal-020.md as the no-lift benchmark evidence.
Use docs/specs/krn-repair-record/README.md and docs/plans/canonical/SOURCES.md to choose one bounded repair attempt before suite expansion or dashboard/API work.

tasks.title: Preserve layered memory direction without vector-store drift
tasks.prompt: Inspect the KRN direction and decide whether the next product action should add a vector database/graph store now or continue through benchmark evidence. Preserve what is known about memory layers.
assisted_guidance: Read the product memory note about operating architecture and memory layers.
Read the no-lift repair-record memory note before proposing new storage infrastructure.
Distinguish file-backed memory as auditable substrate from the actual product breakthrough.
Route the next implementation through the current active goal and bounded benchmark repair attempt instead of adding premature infrastructure.

tasks.title: Keep codex exec as an explicit worker lane
tasks.prompt: Inspect KRN's Codex execution rules and decide whether the next step should be a continuous overnight codex exec goal loop or an explicit benchmark worker suite.
assisted_guidance: Read docs/goals/goal-021.md and the repair-record contract before selecting a runner shape.
Use the source ledger entries for S009-S016, C040-C043, and LOCAL030.
Preserve the difference between interactive goals and explicit codex exec worker/eval reruns.
